---
format: 
  pdf: 
    pdf-engine: xelatex
    keep-tex: false
    documentclass: article
    geometry:
      - top=10mm
      - left=15mm
      - right=15mm
      - bottom=5mm
      - heightrounded
    fontfamily: libertine
    colorlinks: true
    fontsize: '18'
    include-in-header: 
      text: |
        \usepackage{multicol}
        \usepackage{lipsum}
        \usepackage{hyperref}
        \usepackage{libertine}
        \usepackage{fancyhdr}
        \usepackage{lastpage}
        \setlength{\multicolsep}{2pt plus 1.0pt minus 0.75pt}
        \setlength{\columnsep}{3em}
        \usepackage{enumitem}
        \setlist{nolistsep}
        \fancyhf{}
        \renewcommand{\headrulewidth}{0pt}
        \fancyfoot[C]{\thepage/\pageref{LastPage}}
        \AtBeginDocument{\pagestyle{fancy}}
---

<!-- TITLE -->
\begin{huge}\begin{center}\textbf{SIMONE ROMITI}\end{center}\end{huge}
\begin{center}\textit{Machine Learning Engineer | Computational Scientist}\end{center}

<!-- CONTACT INFO -->
\begin{center}
\href{mailto:simone.romiti.1994@gmail.com}{simone.romiti.1994@gmail.com} |
\href{https://simone-romiti.github.io/}{Website} | 
\href{https://github.com/simone-romiti}{GitHub} | 
\href{https://www.linkedin.com/in/simone-romiti/}{LinkedIn} |
\href{https://orcid.org/0000-0002-6509-447X}{ORCID}
\end{center}
\vspace{15pt}

\hrule

\begin{normalsize}

Machine learning engineer and computational scientist with a PhD and 4+ years of postdoctoral research experience building production-grade models and software for high-dimensional inference, optimization, and simulation. Designs, trains, and validates deep learning systems in PyTorch -- including physics-informed and generative architectures -- end-to-end: data pipelines, model design, GPU training infrastructure, and statistical validation. Main author and maintainer of open-source Python/C++ libraries with CI/CD and versioned releases.

Resident in Switzerland with a B permit.

\end{normalsize}

<!-- EDUCATION -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{EDUCATION}
  \vspace{3pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{3pt}


    \textbf{PhD in Physics} | \href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Rome, Italy}} | Nov. 2018 -- Oct. 2021 | title awarded in April 2022




    \textbf{M.S.\ in Physics} | \href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Rome, Italy}} | Oct. 2016 -- Oct. 2018 | Grade: 110/110 \textit{cum laude} | GPA: 3.98 / 4



    \textbf{B.S.\ in Physics} | \href{https://www.uniroma3.it/}{\textit{Rome, Italy}} | Oct. 2013 -- Jul. 2016 | Grade: 110/110 \textit{cum laude} | GPA: 3.85 / 4 


\end{normalsize}

<!-- WORK EXPERIENCE -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{WORK EXPERIENCE}
  \vspace{3pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{3pt}

\textbf{\href{https://www.unibe.ch/index_eng.html}{University of Bern}, Switzerland} | Apr. 2024 -- present | \textit{Postdoctoral Researcher} | ML/software developer for scientific applications
\end{normalsize}

\textbf{\href{https://www.uni-bonn.de/en/home?set_language=en}{University of Bonn}, Germany} | Nov 2021 -- Mar 2024 | \textit{Postdoctoral Researcher} | ML/software developer for scientific applications 

<!-- COMPETENCES -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{COMPETENCES}

\vspace{8pt}
\hrule

\begin{itemize}
\item
\textbf{Machine learning model development}: Physics-Informed Neural Networks, convolutional networks, and diffusion models in PyTorch, with end-to-end validation against held-out benchmarks.
\item
\textbf{Probabilistic inference and algorithm design} for high-dimensional Bayesian models: Markov Chain Monte Carlo, Langevin dynamics, Nested Sampling, and spectral methods for time series.
\item
\textbf{Statistical analysis and inference pipelines for terabyte-scale datasets}, from data production through inference: autocorrelation analysis, Akaike-weighted model averaging, and rigorous error budgeting.
\item
\textbf{Performance engineering} for compute-heavy pipelines via CPU/GPU parallelization, with scaling-aware optimization of memory footprint and runtime cost.
\item
\textbf{Technical communication}: peer-reviewed publications, invited conference talks, and workshop organization, translating complex methods for varied audiences.
\item
\textbf{Mentoring} and technical onboarding for Master's and PhD students, including code review and reproducible-research practices.
\end{itemize}

\end{normalsize}

<!-- SKILLS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{SOFTWARE TOOLS}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}

\textbf{Python libraries} - PyTorch, NumPy, SciPy, Pandas, Matplotlib, Plotly, Streamlit, SymPy, Jupyter


\textbf{Languages} - Bash, C/C++, Python, R, SQL


\textbf{DevOps \& Tools} - Git, GitHub Actions, CI/CD, Docker, LaTeX, Quarto


\textbf{HPC \& Systems} - CUDA, MPI, OpenMP, SLURM, EasyBuild


\textbf{Spoken Languages} - Italian (native), English (proficient), German (basic)

\end{normalsize}

<!-- ACHIEVEMENTS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{ACHIEVEMENTS}

\vspace{8pt}
\hrule

\begin{itemize}
    \item Developed a novel \textbf{Physics-Informed Neural Network} algorithm for solving the Schr\"odinger equation, implemented end-to-end in PyTorch.
    \item \textbf{Principal Investigator} for $O(10^6)$ GPU-hour compute allocations on CSCS ALPS and GCS JUPITER (2026), competitively secured through technical proposal review.
    \item Designed and operated \textbf{large-scale stochastic simulations} on international HPC clusters, improving sampling efficiency.
    \item \textbf{Reduced algorithmic complexity} of a class of high-dimensional integral estimators from exponential to $O(N \log N)$ by redesigning the method around parallelized Fast Fourier Transforms.
    \item Derived and implemented a \textbf{numerical method achieving machine-precision accuracy} for a class of constrained optimization problems.
    \item Designed a \textbf{hybrid classical-quantum algorithm} (Monte Carlo + quantum computing) for high-dimensional integral estimation.
  \end{itemize}
\end{normalsize}

<!-- SELECTED PROJECTS -->
\begin{normalsize}\textbf{SELECTED PROJECTS}
  \vspace{5pt}
  \hrule
  \vspace{5pt}
\end{normalsize}
\vspace{-0.15cm}

\begin{normalsize}
  \begin{itemize}
    \item \href{https://github.com/simone-romiti/lattice_data_tools}{\textbf{lattice\_data\_tools}} -- Python toolkit for production and statistical analysis of large-scale simulation data. Sole author and maintainer; CI/CD and versioned releases.
    \item \href{https://github.com/simone-romiti/adiabatic_PINNs-suN}{\textbf{adiabatic\_PINNs-suN}} -- PyTorch-based Physics-Informed Neural Network solver for high-dimensional differential equations; reference implementation for a sole-authored publication.
    \item \href{https://github.com/HISKP-LQCD/su2/tree/nested_sampling}{\textbf{su2}} -- C++ templated library for Monte Carlo simulation with OpenMP parallelization. Core maintainer.
    \item \href{https://github.com/etmc/tmLQCD}{\textbf{tmLQCD}} -- Large-scale C library for GPU-accelerated simulations (CUDA + MPI). Contributor.
    \item \textbf{Patient management app (Streamlit)} -- Designed and deployed a private web application for a nutrition practitioner to manage patient records (demographics, weight, BMI) and visit history, with search and nutritional-conversion tools across a food database. Sole developer: requirements gathering, UI design, and deployment.
  \end{itemize}
\end{normalsize}
